description: The immediate time, the day we inhabit now. More than a calendar marker, it represents presence, the gift of this particular moment in the cycle of days, encouraging mindful attention to the present.
rationale: The word 'keo' begins with the sharp, present 'ke', suggesting immediate awareness. The vowel hiatus 'e-o' creates an opening from focused attention ('e') to encompassing wholeness ('o'), representing how this day contains all possibilities. The simple CV.V structure reflects the fundamental importance of living in the present moment.
